title: America is about to cede Africa’s space industry to China, and nobody’s talking about it.
summary: The article warns that the U.S. is losing influence in Africa's space industry to China, highlighting a lack of attention to the sector despite policy goals. It suggests the Trump administration should prioritize engagement in this area.

title: Astroscale raises funding to support growth strategy
summary: Astroscale, a satellite servicing company, has secured funding to shift from technology demonstrations to a regular series of missions. The capital infusion will support the company's growth strategy.

title: Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up
summary: Astrobotic will sell to Voyager Technologies to accelerate its growth and meet NASA's lunar base initiative demands. The acquisition aims to scale up operations for lunar lander development.

title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms plans to establish a sovereign LEO satellite constellation in India by leasing broadband capacity from existing satellite networks ahead of its IPO. The move aims to bolster India's independent space capabilities.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to strengthen its position in the U.S. government market. The deal aims to expand MDA Space's access to lucrative contracts in the U.S. space industry.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace raised an additional $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company is developing space-to-Earth transportation and a space environment utilization and recovery platform.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states the space industry is prepared to scale up solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company highlights the need for predictable demand from suppliers despite significant private investment.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability and how hardware designed for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) may fail in the evolving orbital economy. It highlights the differences between orbital regions and their unique demands.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has chosen a new space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of this interaction.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft developed by Tsinghua University will participate in international missions to study the asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. This mission aims to gather scientific data about the asteroid's characteristics and behavior.

title: 'Let's not fool the public': Why moon art should be more realistic in the Artemis age
summary: Experts urge for more realistic depictions of the moon in art as NASA's Artemis program progresses. They warn against misleading representations that could distort public perception.

title: Our expert thinks this pocket-friendly drone is brilliant for beginners, and it's $30 off ahead of Amazon Prime Day
summary: A beginner-friendly drone, the DJI Neo, is being offered at a 15% discount ahead of Amazon Prime Day. The deal is highlighted as an excellent opportunity for new drone flyers.

title: James Webb Space Telescope captures the star-forming clouds of Orion A in stunning detail | Space photo of the day for June 22, 2026
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has captured highly detailed images of the star-forming clouds in the Orion A region, showcasing vibrant colors and unprecedented clarity. This marks a significant achievement in astronomical observation.

title: Launch monster: SpaceX has lofted more satellites than everyone else in history, combined
summary: SpaceX has launched more satellites than all other organizations combined in history, with the gap expected to grow. The company's rapid deployment pace is reshaping the satellite industry.

title: Could we actually terraform Mars? Scientists are trying to find out
summary: Scientists have developed a research blueprint to assess the feasibility of terraforming Mars by warming the planet, aiming to make it habitable for Earth life. The study outlines the potential methods and challenges involved in transforming Mars into a livable environment.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit from California (video)
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched 24 Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California. The mission took place on June 21, 2026.

title: Happy Summer Solstice! Today marks the longest day of the year for the Northern Hemisphere
summary: Today marks the summer solstice, the longest day of the year for the Northern Hemisphere. It also signifies the start of astronomical summer.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 215 — Disclosure Day
summary: Episode 215 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with sci-fi/sci-fact author Dr. David Brin, hosted by Rod Pyle and Susan Karlin. The episode focuses on topics related to space exploration and science fiction.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of activities aboard the space station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space's Terran R second stage has been transported to Stennis Space Center as the company progresses toward its planned 2026 debut of the heavy-lift vehicle. This marks a key milestone in the development of the fully reusable rocket.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten space launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider faced weeks of postponements before scrubbing the mission.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. This follows the recent Flight 12 mission.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission, which will test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This crewed mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams are preparing Booster 20 for cryogenic testing ahead of the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the event, company, or category were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores ESA's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses the development of a versatile robotic arm designed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance operational efficiency and capabilities in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials that can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is leading this effort with innovative approaches.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test underwent a 'bake-out' process to prepare for deployment. The procedure likely involved heating the parachute to remove moisture or contaminants.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, enabling more data downlinks across a wider frequency range. This development supports the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese satellite reentry startup, secured a $40 million Series B funding round, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated high-fidelity quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its quantum networking satellite, Q4S, ahead of a planned 2027 flight. This marks a significant milestone in quantum communication technology development.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of its autonomous orbital vehicle Jackal's Mission X-3 test objectives, marking its full commissioning. The mission cleared all final hurdles, as reported by Payload.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from lunar navigation services to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly support surface missions with its new lander technology.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch its first GEO mission in 2027, following a near-Earth mission scheduled for this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace, a New Zealand-Dutch space transportation company, raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to advance its Aurora spaceplane development and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to bolster sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI anomaly detection technology, currently testing it on terrestrial flat sats with plans to expand into orbit.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract. The company announced the contract award on June 18.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration aims to enhance Norway's disaster response and water resource management.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The deal was announced on June 18 by Stockholm-based EQT.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described the facility as a defining moment for the firm.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a recently formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite offerings. The acquisition follows Lyntris's formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund, led by Jeff Bezos, is investing $26 million in the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite project. The investment is part of a coalition supporting the non-profit Earth Fire Alliance's initiative.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: An interview with former Satellite Executive of the Year Tina Ghataure discusses future developments in the satellite industry. The conversation includes breaking news relevant to the space sector.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for an upcoming robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying the mission to prioritize work on space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy update.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate has unveiled new space cybersecurity research aimed at safeguarding critical infrastructure. This effort is part of the Aerospace SPARTA framework.
